sections.about: This metric measures purchases from related parties as a share of total purchases. It is a Section A 'Openness of Business' related-party transaction disclosure, connected to Principle 1 governance, procurement integrity, and conflict-of-interest controls. A lower value indicates better performance because it suggests a smaller proportion of procurement is sourced from related entities.
sections.performanceAndDrivers: In FY2022-23, SAIL led with the lowest share at 6.87%, followed by Jindal at 8%, JSW at 37.5%, and Tata at 40%. In FY2023-24, SAIL remained the leader at 6.66%, Jindal held at 7%, JSW improved to 34%, and Tata was stable at 40%. In FY2024-25, Jindal improved to rank 1st at 5.11%, SAIL moved to 2nd at 8.60%, JSW improved further to 30.03%, and Tata improved to 37.78%. Tata's consistently high related-party purchase ratio reflects integrated group procurement linkages, captive and associated entities, utilities, shared services, raw material logistics, and downstream or upstream group arrangements. In contrast, SAIL's standalone PSU structure, captive mines within the reporting boundary, and broader public procurement framework reduce related-party purchase concentration. Jindal's improvement in FY2025 to 5.11% is notable, placing it ahead of SAIL in the latest year.
sections.tataPositioning: In FY2024-25, Tata ranks 4th at 37.78%, behind Jindal at 5.11%, SAIL at 8.60%, and JSW at 30.03%. Tata has improved marginally from 40% in FY2022-23 and FY2023-24 to 37.78% in FY2024-25, but the gap to peers remains significant. For internal management, the key issue is demonstrating that related-party procurement is competitively priced, strategically justified, and periodically benchmarked against third-party alternatives. Tata's rank has been consistently 4th across all three years despite numeric improvement.
sections.comparabilityNotes: FY2022-23 values for all four companies are prior-year comparatives reported in their respective FY2023-24 BRSRs. Purchase concentration is structurally affected by whether captive power, logistics, raw-material handling, utilities, shared services, or group procurement entities sit inside or outside the listed entity's standalone reporting boundary.
